text: What was wrong with the man's story?
zh: 这个男人的说辞哪里有问题?

text: 'At the time the murder was committed, I was travelling on the 8 o'clock train to London,' said the man.
zh: 男人说:'案发的时候,我正坐八点的火车去伦敦。'

text: 'Do you always catch such an early train?' asked the inspector.
zh: 警官问:'你一直都赶这么早的火车吗?'

text: 'Of course I do,' answered the man.
'I must be at work at 10 o'clock.
My employer will confirm that I was there on time.'
zh: 我的雇主可以证明我准时到了。'

text: 'Would a later train get you to work on time?' asked the inspector.
zh: 警官问:'坐晚一点的火车也能准时上班吗?'

text: 'I suppose it would, but I never catch a later train.'
zh: '我想是可以的,但我从来不坐晚一点的车。'

text: 'At what time did you arrive at the station?'
'At ten to eight. I bought a paper and waited for the train.'
zh: '七点五十。我买了份报纸,然后等车。'
'你没注意到什么异常情况吗?'

text: 'I suggest,' said the inspector,' that you are not telling the truth.
zh: 警官说:'我认为你没有说实话。'

text: I suggest that you did not catch the 8 o'clock train,
zh: '我认为你根本没坐上八点的火车,'

text: but that you caught the 8.25 which would still get you to work on time.
zh: '而是坐了八点二十五分的车,这班车也能让你准时上班。'

text: You see, on the morning of the murder, the 8 o'clock train did not run at all.
zh: '要知道,案发当天早上,八点的那班车根本就没有发车。'

text: It broke down at Ferngreen station and was taken off the line.'
zh: '它在弗恩格林站抛锚了,被撤出了运营。'
